capabilities.intent: Create a new Warning from an external system, attaching typed fields and recipients
capabilities.business_value: Primary ingestion point — external sources (municipalities, utilities) push alerts into the system
flow: Step 1: Validate Warning (type, profile, sourceRef deduplication) (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:159)
Step 2: Insert Warning row with Status=New (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:18)
Step 3: Attach WarningFields (key/value merge data) (evidence: ServiceAlert.Core/Domain/Warnings/Warning.cs:34)
Step 4: Attach WarningRecipients (KVHX / StdReceiver / explicit phone/email) (evidence: ServiceAlert.Core/Domain/Warnings/Warning.cs:39)
constraints: ProfileId scoping enforced (WarningTypeId restricted by ProfileRole)
SourceRef deduplication: WarningExists check before insert
rules: WarningType may be restricted by ProfileRoleName

capabilities.intent: Retrieve historical Warning records for a profile within a date range
capabilities.business_value: Audit trail and status overview for customer operators
flow: Step 1: Query Warnings filtered by ProfileId + date range (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:280)
Step 2: Return read models with Status (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/WarningService.cs:168)

capabilities.intent: Read WarningTypes available to a profile, optionally filtered by ProfileRole
capabilities.business_value: Determines which alert categories a profile is authorized to send
flow: Step 1: Join WarningTypes with ProfileRoleMappings on ProfileId (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:169)
Step 2: Filter by ProfileRoleName if specified (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:176)
Step 3: Return all types if no role restriction (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/WarningService.cs:173)
constraints: ProfileRoleName restriction is optional — omit to return all types for profile
rules: WarningType can be restricted to specific ProfileRole — access controlled

capabilities.intent: CRUD for WarningTemplates — binds a (Profile + WarningType) pair to a MessageTemplate
capabilities.business_value: Defines which broadcast template is used when a warning of a given type is received by a profile
flow: Step 1: Get templates by profile (optionally filtered by role) (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:188)
Step 2: Get single template by id or (profileId, warningTypeId) (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:215)
Step 3: Insert new template with Profile+WarningType binding (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:231)
Step 4: Update template (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:237)
Step 5: Delete template (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:248)
constraints: WarningTemplate is identified by (ProfileId, WarningTypeId) — unique per profile+type
GetWarningTemplatesByCustomer fetches across all profiles in customer scope (evidence: WarningRepository.cs:204)
rules: WarningTemplate must reference a valid MessageTemplate (Template domain)
TestMode flag controls whether broadcasts go to test gateway

capabilities.intent: Read and write per-profile Warning configuration (night-time windows, default recipient strategy)
capabilities.business_value: Controls when and how broadcasts are sent for each profile's warnings
flow: Step 1: Get WarningProfileSettings by ProfileId (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:262)
Step 2: Update settings (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:268)
Step 3: Create initial settings for new profile (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:274)
Step 4: DTO conversion to DB entity (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Dto/WarningProfileSettingsDto.cs:27)
rules: Default recipient strategy falls back here if Warning has none assigned

capabilities.intent: Determine actual phone/email recipients from WarningRecipient entries (KVHX lookup, StdReceiver, explicit numbers)
capabilities.business_value: Bridges the gap between the external system's knowledge and actual contactable recipients
flow: Step 1: Evaluate recipient strategy flags (ShouldSendToAddress, ShouldSendToOwner, ShouldSendToIncludedNumber) (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Workload/WarningWorkloadProcessor.cs:33)
Step 2: Resolve KVHX recipients via address lookup (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Workload/WarningWorkloadProcessor.cs:35)
Step 3: Build WarningMessageDto with resolved recipients (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/InjectionPoints/Dto/WarningMessageDto.cs:21)
Step 4: Grouped send — one broadcast per SmsGroup (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Messages/WarningMessageSender.cs:160)
constraints: Recipient can be: KVHX address, StandardReceiver/Group, or explicit phone/email (all three can coexist)
rules: Strategy flags from WarningProfileSettings if not set on Warning
Recipients enriched from WarningRecipients + WarningFields tables (evidence: WarningRepository.cs:110)

capabilities.intent: Merge Warning-specific key/value fields into the WarningTemplate body before broadcast
capabilities.business_value: Personalises alert text with incident-specific data (location, time, severity) from the external source
flow: Step 1: Attach WarningFields to Warning (evidence: ServiceAlert.Core/Domain/Warnings/Warning.cs:34)
Step 2: Add fields to WarningMessageDto (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/InjectionPoints/Dto/WarningMessageDto.cs:25)
Step 3: Merge fields into template body during send (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Messages/WarningMessageSender.cs:67)
constraints: Fields are simple key/value pairs (WarningField entity)
Merge happens at send time, not at create time
rules: Field keys must match template tokens

capabilities.intent: Manage the lifecycle state of a Warning through processing stages (New → InProgress → Retry → Done/Failed/Postponed)
capabilities.business_value: Ensures reliable delivery with retry logic and audit trail per warning
flow: Step 1: Fetch unprocessed warnings (Status=New) and mark InProgress (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:75)
Step 2: Fetch and reset postponed warnings (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:43)
Step 3: Update Warning status after processing attempt (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:18)
Step 4: Write status transitions via WarningStatusWriter (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Workload/WarningStatusWriter.cs:17)
Step 5: Classify failed status (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Workload/WarningStatus.cs:30)
constraints: Postponed(7) and PostponedAgain(9) are night-time hold states
rules: Postponed warnings are re-queued at start of each processing run

capabilities.intent: Send admin notification email when a Warning has no resolvable recipients
capabilities.business_value: Prevents silent failure — operator is notified when an alert cannot be delivered to anyone
flow: Step 1: Fetch warnings in no-recipient status (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Repository/WarningRepository.cs:127)
Step 2: Process via NoRecipientWarningWorkloadProcessor (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Workload/NoRecipientWarningWorkloadProcessor.cs:27)
Step 3: Build NoRecipient DTO with fields (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/InjectionPoints/Dto/WarningNoRecipientDto.cs:17)
Step 4: Send admin email (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Messages/WarningMessageSender.cs:268)
constraints: Only warnings with no resolvable recipients trigger this path
rules: Admin email target comes from WarningProfileSettings

capabilities.intent: Batch-process pending Warnings through the full recipient resolution → field merge → broadcast creation cycle
capabilities.business_value: Core execution engine — transforms incoming alert events into delivered broadcasts
flow: Step 1: Reset and re-queue postponed warnings (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Workload/PostponedWarningWorkloadLoader.cs:21)
Step 2: Load unprocessed warnings in chunks (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Workload/WarningWorkloadLoader.cs:22)
Step 3: Process each Warning — resolve recipients, merge fields, create broadcast (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Workload/WarningWorkloadProcessor.cs:37)
Step 4: Write status transitions (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/Workload/WarningStatusWriter.cs:17)
Step 5: Handle no-recipient warnings separately (evidence: ServiceAlert.Services/Warnings/WarningService.cs:56)
rules: Processing is transactional per Warning — failure in one does not block others
Postponed warnings re-enter queue at next run
